name: Deep-Linking Pass

tasks.title: Update cross-references in existing human docs
tasks.description: Update cross-references in existing human docs
tasks.body:
Review every file in docs/humans/ and README.md for references to configuration concepts that should now deep-link to the new config pages. Specific targets:

1. how-it-works.md: mentions models, stages, and pipeline. Link to configuration.md model and pipeline sections.
2. audits.md: mentions audit scopes and configuration. Link to config-reference.md audit-related fields.
3. failure-and-recovery.md: mentions configurable thresholds. Link to config-reference.md failure and retries sections.
4. collaboration.md: mentions git config and worktree settings. Link to config-reference.md git section.
5. CLI pages (config-show.md, config-set.md, config-unset.md, config-append.md, config-remove.md): add links to configuration.md for context on what they modify.
6. task-add.md: link --class flag documentation to task-classes.md.
7. README.md Configuration section: link to config-quickstart.md as the entry point. Update existing links that pointed to old configuration.md anchors.
8. docs/humans/README.md: update any table of contents or navigation to include the new pages.

Any mention of 'three tiers,' 'base/custom/local,' 'config.json,' or specific config field names in any human doc should link to the appropriate new page and section. Do not break existing valid links.

tasks.title: Update CLI command pages with config page links
tasks.description: Update CLI command pages with config page links
tasks.body:
Review all CLI command pages in docs/humans/cli/ that relate to configuration:
- config-show.md: link to configuration.md for three-tier context and config-reference.md for field details
- config-set.md: link to configuration.md for merge semantics explanation
- config-unset.md: link to configuration.md for how null/unset interacts with tiers
- config-append.md: link to configuration.md for array merge behavior
- config-remove.md: link to configuration.md for array field context
- task-add.md: link --class flag to task-classes.md for class details and fallback behavior

Each CLI page should help the user understand what they're modifying by linking to the conceptual docs.

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Deep-Linking Pass is complete and correct

audit_enrichment: Verify every existing doc in docs/humans/ and README.md has been reviewed for stale config links. No broken links should remain. Links to old configuration.md anchors that no longer exist must be updated to point to the correct new page and section.

breadcrumbs.text: Updated cross-references across 15 docs files: fixed 8 broken anchors from configuration.md heading changes, added 10 new deep links to config-reference.md and task-classes.md, added 3 new pages to docs/humans/README.md navigation, linked config-quickstart.md from root README.md
Updated 6 CLI command pages with specific configuration anchor links: config-show, config-set, config-unset, config-append, config-remove, task-add

Audit reviewed 17 files (all deliverables plus 5 CLI pages with collateral anchor fixes). PASS. All 28 anchor cross-references introduced by task-0001 and task-0002 resolve correctly against actual headings in configuration.md, config-reference.md, config-quickstart.md, and task-classes.md. Link text is descriptive and contextually appropriate throughout. Navigation in docs/humans/README.md includes all four new pages. Root README.md Configuration section links to config-quickstart.md as entry point. One pre-existing issue escalated: cli.md:91 links to configuration.md#identity but that heading lives in config-reference.md. Build and tests pass; gofmt issue in internal/daemon/coverage_test.go is pre-existing and unrelated. Quality: thorough, well-executed documentation pass with good attention to anchor specificity.

audit.result_summary: Deep-linking pass updated cross-references across 17 documentation files. Task-0001 fixed 8 broken anchors caused by heading renames in configuration.md, added 10 new deep links from concept pages (how-it-works, audits, failure-and-recovery, collaboration) to config-reference.md field sections, updated navigation in both READMEs, and linked config-quickstart.md as the configuration entry point. Task-0002 sharpened 6 CLI config command pages with specific anchor links to merge semantics, array behavior, and null-deletion rules. All 28 anchor cross-references verified valid against target headings. One pre-existing broken link in cli.md escalated to the parent node.

task-0001.objective: Update cross-references across all human docs to deep-link to the new configuration pages
task-0001.what_happened: Fixed broken anchors in 15 files where configuration.md headings changed (#pipelines→#pipeline-stages, #models→#model-configuration, #three-tiers→#three-tier-directory-structure). Added deep links to config-reference.md sections from how-it-works.md (archive), audits.md (audit, doctor), failure-and-recovery.md (failure, retries, daemon), and collaboration.md (overlap_advisory, logs, git). Added config-reference.md to See Also in all 5 CLI config pages. Linked --class flag in task-add.md to task-classes.md. Added config-quickstart.md entry point to root README.md Configuration section. Added config-quickstart, config-reference, and task-classes to docs/humans/README.md navigation.
went_well: Grep sweep after initial edits caught 5 additional broken anchors in CLI pages (init.md, audit-list.md, audit-run.md, start.md, doctor.md)
No broken builds or test failures from the documentation changes
improvements: Some mentions of config.json in configuration.md itself and in task-classes.md are self-referential and don't need external links, which is correct, but a future pass could add more granular anchor links within those pages
action_items: Verify that cli.md line 91 reference to configuration.md#identity still resolves correctly after any future heading changes

task-0002.objective: Update CLI command pages with specific config page deep links
task-0002.what_happened: Replaced 6 generic configuration.md links with targeted anchor links across all CLI config pages and task-add.md. config-show now points to three-tier-directory-structure and merge-semantics. config-set points to merge-semantics with context about deep-merge vs replacement. config-unset points to rule-3-null-deletes. config-append and config-remove both point to rule-2-arrays-replace-entirely. task-add --class flag now links to using-a-class and framework-fallback anchors, and See Also links to creating-a-custom-class.
went_well: Prior task had already added config-reference.md to See Also sections, so this task only needed to sharpen the configuration.md links to specific anchors
All six edits were clean single-section replacements with no conflicts
improvements: The anchor names depend on heading text in configuration.md; if headings change these links will break silently
action_items: Consider a link-checker CI step that validates internal markdown anchors across docs/humans/
